title: festuca pumila subsp. eskia
intro: Festuca pumila subsp. eskia, also known as Esk's sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of the Festuca pumila plant, a type of grass native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Festuca pumila subsp. eskia include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory ailments.
